title: Design Engineer (Structural)
description:
(A)   Essential Job Functions:

The Design Engineer (Structural) is responsible, but not limited to the following:

Structural Design & Analysis:

  • Develop structural designs for buildings, bridges, tunnels, and other infrastructure projects.
  • Perform load calculations, stress analysis, and material selection to ensure structural integrity.
  • Use software such as STAAD.Pro, ETABS, SAP2000, Tekla, AutoCAD, Revit, or ANSYS for design and analysis.
  • Ensure all designs comply with local and international building codes (e.g., Eurocodes, ACI, AISC, BS, IS codes).

Drafting & Documentation:

  • Prepare technical drawings, blueprints, and specifications for construction teams.
  • Work closely with CAD technicians and BIM modelers to create detailed 2D and 3D structural models.
  • Maintain detailed documentation, including design reports, calculations, and technical notes.

Project Coordination:

  • Collaborate with architects, civil engineers, geotechnical engineers, and contractors to ensure design feasibility.
  • Participate in design reviews, risk assessments, and feasibility studies.
  • Assist in resolving design-related issues during construction and provide technical support on-site.

Quality & Safety Compliance:

  • Ensure that structural designs meet safety, durability, and sustainability standards.
  • Conduct structural inspections and provide recommendations for modifications if necessary.
  • Stay updated with industry trends, materials, and construction techniques.

(B)  Minimum Qualifications/ Job Experience:

  • Minimum Degree holder in Civil/Structural Engineering or Geotechnical Engineering or its equivalent.
  • At least 5 years of experience in the Construction industry.

postedCompany.description:
Obayashi Corporation is one of Japan's leading construction groups, and is also a leading player on the global construction market, known for its innovative technologies for building structures.

Obayashi Corporation (Singapore Office) established its operation in 1965, predating Singapore's history as a nation.  It has since developed into one of the largest offices in Obayashi's worldwide network.  Since the start of its operations in 1965, the Singapore Office has distinguished itself with numerous successful diverse projects such as SIA Building and the Esplanade Bridge.  The reclamation project at Changi East Phase 1A and the Changi International Airport Air Traffic Control Tower were among the first few prominent projects that Obayashi Corporation completed in Singapore.
postedCompany.name: OBAYASHI SINGAPORE PRIVATE LIMITED
